water.name: Snake Range High Lakes
aliases: Great Basin National Park High Lakes

authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Snake Range High Lakes: Open year around. 0 trout, catch and release only. Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Nev. Admin. Code § 503.506(4) — Eastern Region: restrictions on bait and lures
sources.quotedText: Only artificial lures with single barbless hooks may be used on the Snake Range High Lakes and on the south fork of the Humboldt River... Only one single barbless hook may be attached to each hook eye or ring of the lure.

sources.title: NDOW Fisheries Division — Commission General Regulation 528, Motor Restrictions and Revisions Related to Fisheries (LCB File No. R053-25)
sources.quotedText: 4. Only artificial lures with single barbless hooks may be used on the Snake Range High Lakes and on the south fork of the Humboldt River from the access causeway for the Lucky Nugget subdivision upstream to Lee.

sources.title: Reintroducing Bonneville Cutthroat Trout — Great Basin National Park (U.S. National Park Service)
Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lakes layer records for the six named lakes comprising "Snake Range High Lakes" (Great Basin National Park, White Pine County, NV): Stella Lake (gaz_id 861580), Teresa Lake (gaz_id 863340), Baker Lake (gaz_id 858788), Johnson Lake (gaz_id 860224), Brown Lake (gaz_id 859026), Dead Lake (gaz_id 859468)
sources.quotedText: "Snake Range High Lakes" is a regulatory grouping (NBWC CR 25-16) rather than a single GNIS feature, so no individual gaz_id exists for it. Constituent lake coordinates (Web Mercator, converted to lon/lat): Stella Lake (-114.31867, 39.00533), Teresa Lake (-114.31116, 39.00317), Baker Lake (-114.31036, 38.95736), Johnson Lake (-114.29772, 38.94362), Brown Lake (-114.30215, 39.00369), Dead Lake (-114.27397, 38.93592). The file's previous coordinate (-114.303, 38.962) had no GNIS citation and sat about 1.4 km from the centroid of these six named lakes; corrected to that centroid (-114.3023, 38.9748), a representative point spanning the group.

periods.label: Open season, catch and release only

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — White Pine County, Snake Range High Lakes
regulations.humanSummary: The Snake Range High Lakes are open to fishing year-round, catch and release only.
regulations.verbatimText: Open year around. 0 trout, catch and release only.

parameters.counting_rule: No trout may be kept; all trout caught must be released immediately.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — White Pine County, Snake Range High Lakes
regulations.humanSummary: Zero trout may be kept at the Snake Range High Lakes; all trout must be caught and released.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — White Pine County, Snake Range High Lakes
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night at the Snake Range High Lakes.
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: This gear restriction protects the native Bonneville cutthroat trout population and ongoing NPS/NDOW restoration work in the Snake Range high-elevation lakes (Great Basin National Park).

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with a single barbless hook may be used at the Snake Range High Lakes; no bait fishing.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with single barbless hooks may be used on the Snake Range High Lakes... Only one single barbless hook may be attached to each hook eye or ring of the lure.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
